summary: Frontend testing for new InteractiveMapPicker component using OpenStreetMap + Leaflet. All tests passed - no JavaScript errors, components load correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: GoogleMapsLocationPicker.js uses lazy loading (React.lazy) for InteractiveMapPicker - good for performance
InteractiveMapPicker.js properly fixes Leaflet default icon URLs using CDN
Both old API (latitude, longitude, onLocationChange) and new API (currentLocation, onLocationSelect) are supported for backward compatibility
Leaflet CSS is imported correctly in InteractiveMapPicker.js
Dependencies verified: leaflet ^1.9.4 and react-leaflet ^5.0.0 installed
context_for_next_testing_agent: New InteractiveMapPicker component tested. Uses OpenStreetMap + Leaflet (free, no API key needed). Component is lazy-loaded in GoogleMapsLocationPicker.js. Map features: GPS location, click to select, draggable marker. Both CartPage.js and CheckoutPage.js use GoogleMapsLocationPicker component.

homepage_load.details: Homepage loads successfully with Trend Syria content

javascript_errors.details: No JavaScript errors related to map components or imports

map_component_load.details: Leaflet/OpenStreetMap references found in checkout page, no errors

eslint_check.details: No linting issues in GoogleMapsLocationPicker.js or InteractiveMapPicker.js

dependencies.details: leaflet ^1.9.4 and react-leaflet ^5.0.0 installed in package.json
